Upfront, it's all about cooling. And so this center grill is 60% larger than on the standard Dark Horse and these outboard grills are twice as large. There is a huge emphasis on track handling with Dark Horse SC, and there are several changes that we can't see, like changes from steel to forged aluminum suspension components, stiffer springs, geometry tweaks that changed the roll center, and slightly tweaked knuckles. The standard tires are Perelli P ZERO Rs, 305 section up front, 315 width in the rear. But if you upgrade to the optional track package, you get these Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2Rs developed specifically for Dark Horse SC. You also get carbon fiber wheels and carbon ceramic brakes. Brembo provides those and the standard brakes with six piston calipers up front and four pistons in the rear. Overall, that track package reduces unsprung weight by 120 pounds all the way around the vehicle. It also ditches the rear seat for another 30 pounds. Call it 150 pound weight reduction with the track package. The interior is mostly recognizable Mustang parts, but there are a few special touches unique to the Dark Horse SC. All of this carbon fiber trim is standard on the SC and the steering wheel is borrowed from the GTD. In the launch here there's also a track pack special edition that comes with 3D printed titanium ship paddles and gear selector knob. These Ricardo seats are optional. They feature a neat checkered flag pattern in the insert. That's a nod to the fact that this vehicle was developed by Ford Racing. There's also a five-mode traction control system that's pulled from GTD. (energetic music) So there you have it, the Ford Mustang Dark Horse SC. Don't call it a Shelby GT 500, but it's okay if you think of it that way. Now, in addition to these engine specs, there's a few details we don't know, namely the price. Is it more than six figures or less than, I'm curious to find out. We'll know that soon because this vehicle goes on sale in March with deliveries beginning this summer. For more on the Dark Horse SC, along with the latest automotive news and reviews, visit motortrend.com. The GR GT is not a concept — it’s a full production, road-legal sports car from GAZOO Racing (GR), developed alongside the GR GT3 race car and the Lexus LFA Concept.  Built on Toyota’s first all-aluminum body-frame, with carbon-fiber-reinforced panels, the GR GT targets a curb weight of 3,858 lbs or less — with a 45:55 front-to-rear weight balance for optimal handling.     Under the hood: a short-stroke, hot-V 4.0 L twin-turbo V8 mated to a transaxle-integrated electric motor and 8-speed automatic transmission. System output is targeted at 641 hp and 627 lb-ft of torque.  Tires are bespoke Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 — size 265/35 R20 front, 325/30 R20 rear — with 20-inch forged BBS wheels and Brembo brakes for performance-level grip and stopping power.  Inside, the cabin is designed for drivers first: seating position, controls, and visibility were optimized for both everyday driving and track use.
